+- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.

content.content:
415	- Added `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` — subscribes to `AccessibilityInfo.reduceMotionChanged`, with a **session-scoped module-level cache** so only the first mount per app launch pays the async-race cost (reviewer finding, fixed in this commit).
416	- Files changed: `src/design/motion.ts` (new), `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` (new)
417	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (module-only story — no UI surface yet; first real consumer is US-025 ButtonPrimary press-collapse)
418	- Code-reviewer verdict: one Medium (confidence 85) issue fixed in this commit — `useState(false)` default would flash "motion OK" during the async `isReduceMotionEnabled()` call on every mount, briefly running full animations on devices with reduce-motion on. Fix: module-scope `cached: boolean | null` variable, `useState(cached ?? false)`, both the initial resolve and the subscription write back into the cache. First mount per app session still races; every subsequent mount reads synchronously.
419	- Spec gap: story AC says "§6" but the motion presets actually live in docs/design.md §1.4 (§6 is the Component Library Inventory). Followed §1.4 values per the "specs win" rule. No scope change.
420	- Spec gap: story signature is `withReducedMotion(animation, reduced)`; I implemented it as a CONFIG augmenter (first arg is a `WithSpringConfig`/`WithTimingConfig` shape, not a constructed `withSpring(...)` animation object). Reanimated 4 animation objects are opaque workletized closures that can't be introspected for their target value after construction — the config-level augmentation using `ReduceMotion.Always` is the only semantically correct way to produce a no-op without either (a) re-taking the target value as a second arg, or (b) reading private Reanimated internals. Call sites will read: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))`.
421	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
422	  - Reanimated v4 exposes `ReduceMotion.{System,Always,Never}` as a per-animation config flag. `ReduceMotion.Always` snaps the current value to the target on `onStart` and returns complete immediately — no easing, no ticks. Use this instead of rolling your own "if reduced return withTiming(..., {duration: 0})" wrappers.
423	  - React Native has NO synchronous API for `isReduceMotionEnabled`. Any hook that wraps it will race on first mount unless you keep a session-level cache. The cache only needs `let cached: boolean | null = null` at module scope — no cleanup needed because modules don't unmount, and the subscription keeps it fresh.
424	  - `AccessibilityInfo.addEventListener('reduceMotionChanged', cb)` returns an `EventSubscription` with `.remove()` in RN 0.81 (the old `AccessibilityInfo.removeEventListener` is removed). Cleanup pattern is `return () => sub.remove()`.
425	  - When a helper's literal story signature doesn't translate cleanly to the runtime's API shape (as with `withReducedMotion(animation, reduced)` here), the honest move is: (1) implement the semantically equivalent shape that actually works, (2) document the deviation in the file header + progress entry, (3) wire the call-site comment so future consumers know how to call it. Don't force-fit a broken signature just to match the story wording — the story wording is a spec of intent, not of syntax.
426	  - `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` is the preferred pattern for typed preset objects. `as const` narrows to literal types; `satisfies` enforces the shape without widening. Use this everywhere in `src/design/`.
427	---
428

## 2026-04-17 13:10 — US-025
- Added `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx` exporting `ButtonPrimary` (orange CTA) and `ButtonSecondary` (ink-bordered white) over a shared `BaseButton` — Reanimated `useSharedValue` drives a single `useAnimatedStyle` that animates `translateX/Y 0→3`, `scale 1→0.97`, and `shadowOpacity 1→0` in one spring (SPRING_SOFT per §1.4), simulating the neubrutalist shadow-collapse. `onPressIn` fires `Haptics.impactAsync(Light)` per §1.5; disabled state swaps to `colors.hairline` bg + `ink40` border + no shadow + no haptic + no press events; loading state shows an inline `ActivityIndicator` and sets `accessibilityState.busy`.
- Temporary verification harness on `app/index.tsx` renders all four visual states (Primary / Secondary / Disabled / Loading) with testID anchors. The screen will be replaced wholesale by US-031 routing.
- Files changed: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx` (new), `app/index.tsx` (temporary harness), `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-025-rest.png` (new)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp ✓ (iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.3 — rest state captured; pressed-state capture via `simctl io screenshot` scheduled 350ms into `mobile_long_press`(3000ms) produced a visually indistinguishable frame — a 3px translate + opacity fade is below the signal threshold for static PNG verification, so press correctness is relied upon code review + Reanimated pattern adherence)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — reviewer caught that `shadowOffset` is a compound object prop and is NOT animatable through Reanimated `useAnimatedStyle` (the initial implementation silently rendered rest-state shadow during press). Fix: animate `shadowOpacity` 1→0 and keep the base `StyleSheet` `shadowOffset: (3,3)` fixed. Also fixed stale-closure `spring` const by inlining `withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced)` at both press handler call-sites, and added `if (!interactive) return;` guard to `handlePressOut` (defense-in-depth for the disabled flicker edge case).
- Spec gap: story text said "snappy spring" for press feedback but docs/design.md §1.4 line 100 specifies spring.soft — followed the spec verbatim (SPRING_SOFT). Spec gap: `docs/design.md` §4.1 line 722 lists a `variant` prop on `ButtonPrimary` but never defines the variant values anywhere in the doc; left unexpanded — the single orange CTA is the only primary variant in v1. Human should clarify if additional primary variants (e.g. destructive, subtle) are intended before US-042 (apply modal) or US-059 (lister decision UI) ship.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` prop and Reanimated's JS-less path doesn't drive it through `useAnimatedStyle`. Animate `shadowOpacity` (and optionally `shadowRadius`) instead — those are scalars and get interpolated. The visual effect of "shadow collapses into the button on press" is achieved by fading opacity to 0 while translating the content `(0,0) → (3,3)` so the button appears to sink into where the shadow was.
  - The `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` preset typing DOES require explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic widening on `withReducedMotion` calls (readonly literal doesn't structurally satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint under strict mode). US-024's progress note flagged this as a "clarity aid" — clarifying now that it's mandatory. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - Mid-press animation state is below the static-screenshot signal threshold for Marketify's neubrutalist press (3px translate + scale 0.97 + opacity fade). Future primitive stories should capture rest-state only; press correctness is established via code review + Reanimated pattern adherence, not via trying to freeze the spring at peak compression through simctl timing tricks. CampaignCard (US-027) will be more photographable since the hero card is larger — revisit the pressed-state capture approach there.
  - For any primitive with `onPress*` handlers, guard BOTH `handlePressIn` and `handlePressOut` with `if (!interactive) return;` even when `Pressable.disabled` is already set — defense-in-depth for the transient window between interaction mode flips (e.g. a loading state toggling mid-press).
---
